description: When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

messages.content: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.0001, what caveat is documented for Central?

Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will be disconnected. This cycle of connecting and disconnecting continues until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To prevent this unnecessary reconnection, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

description: When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

messages.content: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.0001, what caveat is documented for Hot Patch?

Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

messages.content: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.0001, what caveat is documented for PIM-SM?
Feature Caveat: PIM-SM - Pim Active-Active is not supported on overlay VXLAN SVIs.

description: When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

messages.content: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.0001, what is the caveat related to SNMP?

Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

description: When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

messages.content: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.0001, for HPE Aruba AOS-CX 10.14.0001, what caveat is documented for Certificates?

Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

description: Boundary values for match vni and set local preference in a route-map system cannot be set via the REST API and must be manually configured on the switch via the CLI.

messages.content: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.0001, what is the caveat regarding REST?
Feature Caveat: REST - Boundary values for match vni and set local preference in a route-map system cannot be set via the REST API and must be manually configured on the switch via the CLI.

description: In environments with VRRP or VSX peers, while performing mutual route leaking on the VRRP peers with BGP neighborship established in between and towards the upstream network, the switch will install both routes as ECMP instead of preferring the leaked route. Use route-maps to give lower/higher preference to the routes received from an iBGP peer. For example: Copy !

messages.content: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.0001, what caveat is documented for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - In environments with VRRP or VSX peers, while performing mutual route leaking on the VRRP peers with BGP neighborship established in between and towards the upstream network, the switch will install both routes as ECMP instead of preferring the leaked route. Use route-maps to give lower/higher preference to the routes received from an iBGP peer.

description: Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

description: Symptom: IGMP/MLD configuration changes do not take effect when the hpe-mgmdd process is flooded with IGMP/MLD control packets. Scenario: In a situation where IGMP or MLD control packets (Joins or Leaves) are part of a loop, or if misbehaving clients send continuous streams of IGMP/MLD control packets, the hpe-mgmdd daemon's packet queue can become continuously full. Workaround: Restart the MGMD daemon using the command systemctl restart hpe-mgmdd.

description: Symptom: DHCP relay does not change the source IP address of the DHCP discover frame. Scenario: This issue occurs if the DHPCP client does not use 0.0.0.0 as the source IP, for example. when the DHCP packet is generated by the client with a valid private IP as source IP. Workaround: Use the source interface configuration.

description: Symptom: An Interface VLAN configuration is not getting synced to secondary switch. Scenario: This issue can occur n a pair of switches in VSX configuration, when the user creates an Interface VLAN with VSX-Sync configuration in primary switch, then the user creates the same Interface VLAN on secondary, but the configuration is not synced. Workaround: Once VSX-Sync has been enabled on primary Interface VLAN, wait ~15 seconds before creating the Interface VLAN on the secondary interface. Another workaround is to first create the Interface VLAN on the secondary and then enable VSX-Sync on the primary Interface VLAN. (There is no need to wait in this second workaround).

description: Symptom: The VSX software upgrade software version is not properly validated when upgrading using TFTP. Scenario: When upgrading using TFTP, it is possible to load mismatched software versions, resulting in a VSX upgrade failure and a VSX software mismatch between the primary and secondary VSX nodes. Workaround: Confirm that software image versions are compatible. Software upgrades using boot banks rather than TFTP will perform a proper version check before completing the upgrade.

description: Symptom: IGMP/MLD configuration changes do not take effect when the hpe-mgmdd process is flooded with IGMP/MLD control packets. Scenario: In a situation where IGMP or MLD control packets (Joins or Leaves) are part of a loop, or if misbehaving clients send continuous streams of IGMP/MLD control packets, the hpe-mgmdd daemon's packet queue can become continuously full. Workaround: Restart the MGMD daemon using the command systemctl restart hpe-mgmdd.

workaround: Restart the MGMD daemon using the command systemctl restart hpe-mgmdd.

messages.content: Which category does Bug 215677 belong to?
IGMP (Bug ID 215677): This bug belongs to the IGMP category.

description: Symptom: An Interface VLAN configuration is not getting synced to secondary switch. Scenario: This issue can occur n a pair of switches in VSX configuration, when the user creates an Interface VLAN with VSX-Sync configuration in primary switch, then the user creates the same Interface VLAN on secondary, but the configuration is not synced. Workaround: Once VSX-Sync has been enabled on primary Interface VLAN, wait ~15 seconds before creating the Interface VLAN on the secondary interface. Another workaround is to first create the Interface VLAN on the secondary and then enable VSX-Sync on the primary Interface VLAN. (There is no need to wait in this second workaround).

description: Symptom: The VSX software upgrade software version is not properly validated when upgrading using TFTP. Scenario: When upgrading using TFTP, it is possible to load mismatched software versions, resulting in a VSX upgrade failure and a VSX software mismatch between the primary and secondary VSX nodes. Workaround: Confirm that software image versions are compatible. Software upgrades using boot banks rather than TFTP will perform a proper version check before completing the upgrade.
